availability.p: © Duke Databank of Documentary Papyri. This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 License.

revisionDesc.change: Vote - Accept-Straight-to-Finalization - Adopt the suggestion made by Gonis in reply to Kaltsas.
General - We could then put <:[ταρι]χ̣ηρὰ(?)=PN J. D. Sosin (from image)|ed|[δὲ σκ]ληρὰ:>: ταρι(χ) would occupy about the same space as δὲ σκ.

Vote - DEFER - ταρι]χ̣ηρά is good, but rejecting ἡμᾶς in l. 9 is too radical for me. (A completely e.g. restoration: φάσκοντες μόνον δεῖν ἔχειν ἡμᾶς ταριχηρὰ κρέατα, ἔχειν `δ̣[ὲ δ]ι᾽ αὐτῶν´: ‘they said we were only supposed to have pickled meat, and to have that through their agency’). What might be a better way of combining the new reading with what should be retained of Edgar’s text? Change punctuation and add question marks?

Vote - AcceptText - I'd be inclined to accept <:[.?][ταρι]χ̣ηρὰ(?)=PN J. D. Sosin (from image)|ed|ἡμᾶς [δὲ σκ]ληρὰ:> in lin9, even if it's hard to think that ἡμᾶς might have belong to Edgar's imagination.

Submit - r1 and v22: CSAD digital image shows Σεῶτι not Σεσῶτι. On Sesos, Pap.Lugd.Bat. 21 p.416: “(Eg.?; hapax legomenon)”. For Seos see: P.Cair.Zen. 1 59133.5; 3 59339.3; 3 59450.2; 4 59656.10; P.Zen.Pestm. 63.29. => <:Σεῶτι=PN J. D. Sosin (CSAD digital image)|ed|Σεσῶτι:>
r3: CSAD image shows τὴν corr. from ταν. => <:<:τὴν|subst|ταν:>=PN J. D. Sosin (CSAD digital image)|ed|τὴν:>
r9: “σκ]ληρὰ κρέα” seems dubious; I find no other “tough/hard meat”. But ed notes, “The writer announces that certain persons, whom I conjecture to have been the farmers of the τετάρτη ταρίχου (see no. 59206), have taken him to prison after searching and sealing up the house and after carrying off a quantity of meat.” This suggests an alternative. If the right foot of the Λ (σκ]ληρὰ) is construed as the right foot of a Χ, then we can consider “ταρι]χ̣ηρὰ κρέα” (cf. P.Petr. 3 58(a) 2: κρέων ταλ̣ι̣χηρῶν; SB 22 15708.62-63: ταριχηρὰ | κρέα). The fragment bearing the starts of lin.9 (ἡμᾶς [) and 10 (ἔχ[ειν]) had already been lost between Edgar’s first transcription and publication and does not not appear in the digital image; it is difficult to say how secure the readings are or how ταρι]χ̣ηρὰ would fit. “ἡμᾶς [ταρι]χ̣ηρὰ” would trip on syntax (nothing to connect the two infinitives) and space (ἡμᾶς [δὲ ταρι]χ̣ηρὰ would be a tight fit). A conservative encoding could be : <:[.?][ταρι]χ̣ηρὰ(?)=PN J. D. Sosin (CSAD digital image)|ed|ἡμᾶς [δὲ σκ]ληρὰ:> . This is far from solid, and I hesitate to float it, unless eds are convinced, but it seems worth recording in the submission notes.
changed schema; added xml:space=preserve; indented; moved title/@n to idno
Converted from TEI P4 (EpiDoc DTD v. 6) to P5 (EpiDoc RNG schema)

div.ab:
νις ΣεῶτιΣεσῶτι χαίρειν.
οἱ ἐπὶ τῆς τετάρτης
εἰσβιασάμενοι τὴνταντὴν οἰ
κίαν ἡμῶν παρεσφρα
γίσαντο καὶ τὰ α
τῶν κρεῶν π οἷοι ἦσαν
ἐξενέγκαι, φάσκοντες
ν ἔχειν,
ἡμᾶς ταριχηρὰδὲ σκληρὰ κρέα
ἔχειν ἀντὶ αὐτῶν, καὶ ἐμέμη τε εἰς φυλα
κὴν ἀπαγηόχασιν.
καλῶς οὖν ποιήσεις ἐν
τυχὼν Ζήνωνι ὑπὲρ
τούτων. ὡσαύτως δὲ
καὶ Ἀχομμνεῦις ὁ πα
ρὰ Ζήνωνος ἄπηκται
πρὸς τὰ ἁλικὰ ὑπὸ τοῦ
κωμογραμματέως
Ὥρου.
ἔρρωσο.
ἔτους λε, Μεσορὴ ιδ.
